course_description.overview: Iconic road hill climb from base in Chom Thong to summit of Doi Inthanon, Thailand's highest peak at 2565m. Brutal ascent with steep gradients, especially in the second half.

key_segments.name: Initial Climb
key_segments.description: Manageable gradients building to the base of steeper sections.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Arguably Thailand's toughest climb due to relentless steep gradients after the first half, with sections up to 20%. Known to break even experienced riders.

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Chiang Mai Airport (CNX), then drive or shuttle to Chom Thong. Mild traffic on double-lane highway.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and guesthouses in Chom Thong and Chiang Mai. Book early for event weekend.
logistics.registration_details: Cannot register via Ahotu; check official site. 17th edition in 2026.
logistics.bike_logistics: Event provides downhill transport post-race due to descent restrictions. Fly with bike to Chiang Mai.
logistics.local_considerations: Excellent organization with no reported accidents. Mild traffic, safe roads. Expect tropical climate turning cooler at altitude.

final_verdict.one_liner: Brutal Thai hill climb masterpiece to the roof of Thailand.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong climbers seeking epic elevation and prestige in Southeast Asia.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the challenge, scenery, and flawless organization.
pro_tips: Pace conservatively; save for the 14-16% final 10km.
Use power meter guides for the gradients.
